other_observers: Bill Schmoker, and a host of others; Glenn Walbek first found and identified the bird on the 11th.
duration: off and on for about 40 minutes
specific_location: on ice along west side of Cherry Creek Reservoir
description: A pale gull larger than Ring-billed Gulls and obviously smaller than Herring Gulls in the vicinity, the bird had dark yellow eyes, a pinkish-gray bill with a distinct black tip accounting for about 40% of its length, and bright pink legs. The mantle was pale gray, paler than that of any of the Ring-billed and Herring gulls nearby. The forehead, face, chin, throat, and breast were white and unmarked. The white crown area around the eyes were very lightly streaked with pale brown, as was the nape. The rest of the underparts were smudgy pale brown.  The rump was white and contrasted with the gray mantle and the off-white-based, pale brown tail. The wing coverts and tertials were all immature-type feathers, being white with distinct pale-medium brown barring. The primaries on the folded wing were essentially white with only-just-visible pale brown centers. The tail tip fell between the tips of P7 and P8.Behaviors: loafingCall: none heardPlumage: Second Basic
